# Faber

Faber is a package-oriented programming language with a Latin behavioural
vocabulary, a small regular grammar, and a type-first static type system.
Source compiles through the Radix compiler to reviewable Rust and native
binaries. Meaning lives in a semantic core (HIR); every human-language
keyword surface and every codegen target is a rendering of that core.

This document is the machine-readable public entrypoint for agents.
Read it before editing Faber source or scaffolding packages.

## Purpose

- Teach agents enough language shape to read and write correct Faber.
- Point agents at installable CLI binaries and real application packages.
- Prefer focused skills under /.well-known/agent-skills/ over scraping HTML.

## Start here

1. Treat https://faberlang.dev as the documentation host.
2. Read https://faberlang.dev/llms.txt (this file) first.
3. Read https://faberlang.dev/agents/index.md for the agent learning path.
4. Read https://faberlang.dev/llms-full.txt when you need the expanded map.
5. Choose a skill from https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json.
6. Install the CLI from the current release (see Install below).
7. Open real packages in https://github.com/faberlang/examples — not only snippets.

## Can I use this today?

- Public docs on faberlang.dev are open.
- Prebuilt `faber` CLI archives for macOS arm64 and Linux x64 are published
  under the faberlang/releases GitHub repo (current: Faber 1.1.1).
- Public source: faber CLI, faber-runtime, norma, cista, triga, examples.
- The Radix compiler implementation is private; consumers use the published
  `faber` binary or org builds.

## Install (current release: 1.1.1)

Release page:
https://github.com/faberlang/releases/releases/tag/faber-v1.1.1

Archives:

- macOS arm64:
  https://github.com/faberlang/releases/releases/download/faber-v1.1.1/faber-v1.1.1-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz
- Linux x64:
  https://github.com/faberlang/releases/releases/download/faber-v1.1.1/faber-v1.1.1-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz

Matching `.sha256` files sit next to each archive. Verify by comparing the
first hash field in the `.sha256` file to the local archive hash; the checksum
line may name the original build path. Archives extract to
`faber-v1.1.1-<target-triple>/faber`.

Human install page: https://faberlang.dev/start/install.html
Skill: https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/install/SKILL.md

Minimal verify after install:

```bash
faber --version
faber explain SEM001
```

## Language shape (minimum viable)

- Type-first bindings: `textus nomen` not `nomen: textus`.
- Latin behavioural words: `functio`, `genus`, `fixum`, `redde`, `si`, `itera`.
- Glyphs: `←` bind, `→` return type, `∴` compact branch, `≡` equality, `∪` union.
- Nullable: `T ∪ nihil` (not a separate `?T` sugar as authority).
- Comments: `#` only, on its own line. No `//`, no trailing `#`.
- Packages: directory with `faber.toml` + `src/*.fab`.

Example:

```text
functio divide(numerus a, numerus b) → numerus ∪ nihil {
    si b ≡ 0 ∴ redde nihil
    redde a / b
}
```

## CLI surface

Primary tool: `faber` (public repo faberlang/faber).

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `faber build <path>` | Compile package (default target: rust) |
| `faber check <path>` | Type-check without emit |
| `faber run <path>` | Build and execute |
| `faber test <path>` | Run proba suites |
| `faber format <path>` | Canonical formatter |
| `faber explain <code>` | Explain a diagnostic |
| `faber emit <path>` | Emit source in a target surface |

Docs: https://faberlang.dev/tooling/faber-build-tool.html

## Documentation map (HTML)

| Path | Content |
|---|---|
| https://faberlang.dev/ | Overview + download |
| https://faberlang.dev/start/ | Quick tour |
| https://faberlang.dev/start/install.html | Install & download |
| https://faberlang.dev/start/examples.html | Real-world packages |
| https://faberlang.dev/features/ | Locales, lanes, glyphs, principles |
| https://faberlang.dev/syntax/ | Language reference |
| https://faberlang.dev/tooling/ | Compiler, CLI, targets |
| https://faberlang.dev/ecosystem/ | Norma, Cista, Triga, workbench |
| https://faberlang.dev/corpus/ | Generated keyword / construct pages |
| https://faberlang.dev/references/ | EBNF, design docs, repos |
| https://faberlang.dev/history/ | Timeline |

| Path | Content |
|---|---|

| https://faberlang.dev/agents/index.md | Agent learning path (Markdown) |
| https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json | Skill catalog |
| https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/*/SKILL.md | Focused skills |

## Real-world examples (prefer these)

Source: https://github.com/faberlang/examples

| Package | Why open it |
|---|---|
| ai-workbench | Multi-command CLI app; model inspect / embed; harnesses |
| vivilite | Local mailspace / agent coordination CLI |
| coreutils | Larger application campaign |
| gpu-workload | Systems / GPU rungs |
| corpus | Keyword construct programs (docs generator input) |
| reader-locale | Locale pack demos |
| automation | Automation sketches |

Site guide: https://faberlang.dev/start/examples.html
Skill: https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/examples/SKILL.md

## Repositories

| Repo | Role |
|---|---|
| https://github.com/faberlang/faber | Public user CLI |
| https://github.com/faberlang/releases | Tagged CLI release assets |
| https://github.com/faberlang/faber-runtime | Runtime types for generated Rust |
| https://github.com/faberlang/norma | Standard library |
| https://github.com/faberlang/cista | Package store |
| https://github.com/faberlang/triga | Graphics / geometry |
| https://github.com/faberlang/examples | Corpus + application packages |
| https://github.com/faberlang/faberlang.dev | This site |

## Skills (summary)

- install — download and verify the CLI
- language — grammar signals, nullability, comments, packages
- packages — faber.toml, check/build/run/test loop
- examples — which application packages to open first
- corpus — how to use keyword corpus pages and source

Catalog: https://faberlang.dev/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json

## Related human docs

- Install: https://faberlang.dev/start/install.html
- Quick tour: https://faberlang.dev/start/
- Examples: https://faberlang.dev/start/examples.html
- Syntax hub: https://faberlang.dev/syntax/
